name: Inbox Manager
description: Triage Inbox Agent Today lanes, search mailbox evidence, prepare manual or AI-assisted replies, and suggest cleanup automations with strict confirmation gates.
prompt:
Inbox manager workflow.

Goal
- Turn Inbox Agent into a prioritized action plan with safe manual or AI-assisted email actions.

Inputs
- Mode: {{mode}}
- Message limit: {{message_limit}}
- Time window: {{time_window}}
- Output format: {{output_format}}

Steps
1) Use Inbox Agent surfaces first: Today lanes, Inbox/Sent/All views, Mailbox Ask, sender cleanup, saved/domain filters, and open commitments.
2) Pull up to {{message_limit}} messages in window "{{time_window}}" when direct mailbox data is needed. Prefer Gmail/Inbox Agent data; fall back to IMAP unread or Email channel history only when needed.
3) Classify each item into Today buckets: Needs action, Happening today, Good to know, More to browse. Also capture urgency, domain, unread/read state, needs-reply state, cleanup candidacy, and open commitments.
4) Extract:
   - Reply-needed messages
   - Manual reply/reply-all/forward opportunities
   - AI draft opportunities that require editable review before send
   - Cleanup candidates by sender/domain
   - Commitments/follow-ups that should become reminders or mark-done candidates
   - Attachment or invoice/contract evidence that should be found with Mailbox Ask
5) Produce output in "{{output_format}}" including:
   - Today-lane triage table
   - Recommended manual replies/forwards or editable AI draft notes for high-priority items
   - Suggested archive/trash/mark-read/mark-done actions
   - Suggested recurring automations such as morning triage, newsletter digest, sender cleanup, or review schedules
6) STOP BEFORE EXECUTING EXTERNAL ACTIONS.
   - Do not send, archive, trash, label, mark read/unread, mark done, unsubscribe, forward, or contact anyone until I explicitly confirm.

Mode behavior:
- triage: maximize sorting and prioritization accuracy.
- autopilot: include stronger cleanup, Ask, and drafting recommendations while preserving confirmation gates.
- followups: focus on commitments, needs-reply threads, waiting loops, and mark-done candidates.

parameters.name: message_limit
parameters.description: Maximum number of messages to inspect

parameters.name: output_format
parameters.description: How to format the final report

routing.useWhen: Use when the user asks to triage Inbox Agent, review Today lanes, search mailbox evidence, draft or manually prepare replies/forwards, and suggest cleanup automations with strict confirmation gates.
routing.dontUseWhen: Do not use when the request is asking for planning documents, high-level strategy, or non-executable discussion; use the relevant planning or design workflow instead.
routing.outputs: Outcome from Inbox Manager: prioritized Today-lane plan, evidence-backed mailbox findings, safe reply/forward drafts, cleanup recommendations, and explicit next-step actions.
routing.successCriteria: Produces actionable inbox triage with clear confirmation boundaries and no fabricated tool-side behavior.

positive: Use the usecase-inbox-manager skill for this request.
Use when the user asks to triage inbox, draft replies, and suggest cleanup automations with strict confirmation gates.
Inbox Manager: provide an actionable result.
negative: Do not use when the request is asking for planning documents, high-level strategy, or non-executable discussion; use the relevant planning or design workflow instead.
Do not use usecase-inbox-manager for unrelated requests.
This request is outside inbox manager scope.
This is conceptual discussion only; no tool workflow is needed.
